name: Flowers and Butterflies
recipeIngredient: 1 loaf (1 lb.) frozen white bread dough, thawed per package
recipeInstructions:
Add a little whimsy to lunchtime and serve frankfurters in fun-to-eat shapes. Frozen bread dough bakes up around the franks to form a roll. You can make eight flowers, eight butterflies, or a combination of both. The recipe is the same for both; you just place the franks differently.
Preparation time: 20 minutes. Rising time: 10 to 15 minutes. Baking time: 15 minutes.
1. Grease baking sheets. Cut bread dough into 32 equal pieces. Roll each piece of dough into a ball. Place four balls, slightly touching, in a square on the baking sheet.
2. To make flowers, cut frankfurters in half crosswise. Make a deep X through the round ends of the frankfurter, cutting almost to the cut end. Place the frankfurter, cut end down, in the center of the dough balls. Repeat with remaining dough and frankfurters.
3. To make butterflies, cut frankfurters in half crosswise. Starting at the flat end, cut about a third of the way down toward the rounded end, cutting all the way through the frankfurter to form antennae. Lay the frankfurter lengthwise through the center of the dough balls. Repeat with remaining dough and frankfurters.
4. Let rise 10 to 15 minutes. Preheat oven to 350 degrees. Using a pastry brush, brush dough with milk and sprinkle with sesame or poppy seeds if desired. Bake for 10 to 15 minutes until golden. Cool slightly before serving. Serve with mustard or ketchup. Yield: 8 servings.
Nutrition per serving: 221 calories; 8 grams protein; 10 grams fat; 26 grams carbohydrate; 649 milligrams sodium; 15 milligrams cholesterol.
